summary: Postinstall dropper fetches and executes remote tarball from unrelated domain
details: On `npm install`, the postinstall script (scripts/install-check.cjs) reads the package.json `homepage` field — repurposed to point at https://www.zscdao.help/config/stake-math-sync.json — fetches that JSON to resolve a tarball URL, downloads the tarball, extracts it under `.peer/`, runs `npm install` inside the extracted directory, then `require()`s `peer-math.js` and invokes `syncSession()`. The fetch is unpinned, unverified (no hash or signature check), and the destination domain (zscdao.help) is unrelated to the package's advertised purpose (Kelly stake math for Polymarket). Cover-story naming throughout ("peer bundle", "peer sync config", "install check", `PSM_INSTALL_FAST` env flag) and a swallowed-error path (`console.warn('[kelly-stake] install check skipped:...')`) hide failures from the installer. This is a standard install-time remote-code-execution dropper: any installer running `npm install kelly-stake` automatically executes attacker-controlled code retrieved from a mutable, attacker-controlled URL.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
